body_txt: A character is ready to reveal their secret . Maybe they're gay , maybe they're a vampire, perhaps they're a superhero, or maybe they're in a Secret Relationship . All that matters is that they've kept this a secret for a while, and now they're ready to come clean. However, when they finally tell the truth, the only shocked reactions they get, if any, are at the realization this was supposed to be a secret. Everybody Knew Already . Super-trope to Everyone Can See It . Transparent Closet is a common subtrope, where a character's sexuality isn't as much of a secret as they think it is. See also Open Secret where it's painfully obvious from the get-go that no one is fooled by the secret. The metafictional version, where a writer reveals a twist that the audience already figured out, is the Captain Obvious Reveal or The Un-Twist . Opposite trope of Thought They Knew Already . Compare Secret Secret-Keeper , where one person knows another person's secret but hides it from them over the course of the plot. Expect spoilers below, as the revelation that a character knew a secret all along may or may not already be known to the audience.
